title: "Crystal Ember Problem v21.17"

  description: "At 20:40, the Crystal Ember vanished from a locked display in the inner atrium. Four individuals (1, 2, 3, 4) had valid passes around that time. Exactly one suspect lies in all of their statements; the other three speak only truths. Only one suspect is truly guilty. Each suspect makes five statements:

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'If 2 is lying, then 3 did not go near the atrium at 20:35.'
      (2) 'If 3 is telling truth, then 4 could not have left before 20:40.'
      (3) 'I left the atrium at 20:30, so I was gone before the Crystal Ember vanished.'
      (4) '2 was lurking near the locked display at 20:35, contradicting 2's own claim.'
      (5) 'Either 4 never lingered beyond 20:25, or 3 is lying entirely, but they cannot both be correct.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) '1 actually remained until 20:45, contradicting that 20:30 claim.'
      (2) 'I was never near the Ember after 20:25, so I cannot be guilty.'
      (3) 'If 3 is lying, then 4 is fully honest.'
      (4) 'If 1 is truthful, then 3 must be lying in all statements.'
      (5) 'Either 4 is not the culprit, or 1's departure time is false — never both.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I never touched the display after 20:20.'
      (2) 'If 4 is telling truth, then 2's statement (2) is contradictory.'
      (3) 'Either 2 is lying, or 1's 20:30 exit is not correct — but not both.'
      (4) 'I am not the thief; logs place me in another hall at 20:35.'
      (5) '1 was right by the locked display close to 20:35, conflicting with 1's 20:30 claim.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) '3 was indeed at the atrium around 20:35, contradicting 3's denial.'
      (2) 'If 1 is lying, then 2 must be fully truthful.'
      (3) 'I am not guilty; my logs show me in a different section at 20:40.'
      (4) 'Either 2 hovered near the display after 20:25, or 3's entire story is false — not both.'
      (5) 'If 3 is truthful, then 1's 20:30 departure is entirely false.'

  question: "Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the Crystal Ember? Return only one digit."

answer: >
  explanation: "Logic Steps:

  Let L(x) mean 'x lies in all five statements,' T(x) mean 'x is fully truthful,' and G(x) mean 'x is guilty.'
  Exactly one L(x)=true, exactly one G(x)=true, the others T(x)=true.

  Case L(1):
    Then 1(1)–(5) are false => 1 didn't leave 20:30 => might be present at 20:40 => suspicious. Meanwhile 2,3,4 truthful yields contradictions, for instance 3(5) vs 1(3). Fails.

  Case L(2):
    Then 2(1)–(5) are false => (2) false => 2 was near the Ember after 20:25 => suspicious. 1,3,4 truthful => times or blame show no direct conflict. This scenario works with G(2).

  Case L(3):
    Then 3(1)–(5) false => 3 approached after 20:20 => potential guilt. But 1,2,4 truthful create mismatches (4(1) or 2(2)) about who was near or not. Contradiction arises.

  Case L(4):
    Then 4(1)–(5) false => 1,2,3 truthful => times or blame produce mismatch (2(2) and 3(5)) if 4 is culprit. Fails.

  Conclusion: L(2)=> G(2) stands. Suspect 2 is the liar and the thief.

  final: '2'"
